title: Scenarios Canvas (Hoskins)
description: L2-born Hoskins scenarios that carry user context L2->L5. Hoskins's model has THREE elements — Motivation, Persona, Simulation (corrected 2026-07-01; an earlier in-repo model added a fabricated 4th 'Means' — 'means' folds into the simulation). Permissive by design (additionalProperties: true): pins the load-bearing core (id, status enum, provenance shape) and leaves the element blocks flexible so /user-interview writes them and /canvas-health step 8b does the semantic 3-element completeness check (schema validates shape; canvas-health drives the corrected-model semantics). Legacy instances may still carry a 4-block persona/means/motive/simulation structure; the permissive schema validates both while 8b flags the distortion. Added v0.55.3 (theory-fidelity B7): the canvas had real instances but no schema.

persona.description: Hoskins element: who the person is (role, context, constraints, goals).

motivation.description: Hoskins element: what the person is trying to accomplish and why (JTBD link). Corrected model; legacy files may carry 'motive' instead.

simulation.description: Hoskins element: the narrative tying it together (a specific, testable story). 'How they interact' (legacy 'means') lives inside the simulation.

motive.description: Legacy field (pre-2026-07-01 4-block model) — superseded by 'motivation'. Kept in the schema so historical instances validate; new scenarios should use 'motivation'.

means.description: Legacy field (pre-2026-07-01 4-block model) — 'Means' is NOT a Hoskins element; fold this into the simulation. Kept so historical instances validate.
